chapter_title.en: Chapter 26 – The Penalty (Hadd) of the slanderer

text: فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِرَجُلٍ يَا ابْنَ الْفَاعِلَةِ يَعْنِي الزِّنَى قَالَ إِنْ كَانَتْ أُمُّهُ حَيَّةً شَاهِدَةً ثُمَّ جَاءَتْ تَطْلُبُ حَقَّهَا ضُرِبَ ثَمَانِينَ جَلْدَةً وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ غَائِبَةً انْتُظِرَ بِهَا حَتَّى تَقْدَمَ فَتَطْلُبَ حَقَّهَا وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ قَدْ مَاتَتْ وَ لَمْ يُعْلَمْ مِنْهَا إِلَّا خَيْرٌ ضُرِبَ الْمُفْتَرِي عَلَيْهَا الْحَدَّ ثَمَانِينَ جَلْدَةً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Al Hakam Al A’ama and Hisham Bin Salim, from Ammar Al Sabaty,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said regarding a man who said to a man, ‘O son of the (sexually) active’, meaning the adultery. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If his mother was alive, present, then she comes seeking her right, he would be whipped eighty lashes; and if she was absent, she would be awaited with until she proceeds, so she seeks her right; and if she had died and nothing was known from her except for the goodness, the fabricator would be whipped, upon him being eighty lashes’.
